sections.description: End-to-end integration test suite for the lil-olt-metrics system. Exercises the complete flow from OTLP metric ingestion through translation, storage, and Prometheus-compatible query API responses. Uses an in-process test server with ephemeral ports to validate the entire system as a cohesive unit.
sections.purpose: Validate that the OTLP ingest, metric translation, storage, and Prometheus query layers work correctly together. Tests end-to-end scenarios for various metric types (gauges, counters, histograms), storage backends (in-memory and filesystem), protocol formats (gRPC, HTTP Protobuf, HTTP JSON), and operational concerns like concurrency, persistence, and error handling.
sections.functionality:
- Provides `startTestServer()` helper that creates a full in-process server with gRPC ingest, HTTP ingest, and Prometheus query API on ephemeral ports
- `testServer` struct manages server lifecycle with cleanup via `t.Cleanup()`
- `grpcClient()` creates authenticated gRPC clients for ingest testing
- Helper functions for query execution: `instantQuery()` for point-in-time queries and `httpGet()` for generic HTTP queries
- Query response parsing helpers: `queryResult`, `instantData`, `vectorSample` structs
- OTLP metric construction helpers: `strAttr()` for string attributes
- Comprehensive test scenarios covering: gauge/counter/histogram ingestion, label/series APIs, metric naming translation, delta conversion, concurrent ingestion/query, rate calculations over time, filesystem persistence with WAL replay, malformed payload rejection, invalid PromQL detection, and build info endpoint
sections.strengths:
- True end-to-end testing eliminates integration gaps between layers
- Ephemeral port allocation allows parallel test execution without port conflicts
- In-process server avoids flaky network issues and simplifies debugging
- Comprehensive coverage of metric types, protocols, and storage backends
- Built-in race detection support via standard Go test flags
- All tests use `t.Parallel()` for efficient execution
- Clear test names and organization make behavior intentions obvious
- Validates real-world scenarios like concurrent load and WAL replay
sections.weaknesses:
- Tests are slow because they start full servers for each test (mitigated by `t.Parallel()`)
- Duplicate test server setup code across tests; could benefit from a test fixture factory or parameterized approach
- Limited error injection testing—edge cases in specific layers not isolated
- Remote backend integration tests (Prometheus Remote Write, VictoriaMetrics) are marked TODO and would require external services
- Cannot detect integration issues that only manifest in distributed deployments
- Query timeout test is marked as TODO
- No performance/load benchmarking
sections.test_coverage: Full integration coverage across all major system components: ingest layer (gRPC and HTTP handlers), metric translation (OTLP to Prometheus format conversion), storage layer (in-memory and filesystem with WAL), query layer (Prometheus API compatibility). Covers: gauge/counter/histogram types, delta temporality conversion, multiple ingestion protocols (gRPC, HTTP Protobuf, HTTP JSON), label/series/metric metadata APIs, range queries with rate calculations, error handling, and filesystem persistence. Coverage gaps: remote backend integration (Prometheus, VictoriaMetrics), timeout scenarios, performance characteristics.
sections.dependencies: External: OpenTelemetry (`go.opentelemetry.io/proto/otlp`) for OTLP protocol buffers, Prometheus (`github.com/prometheus/prometheus`) for query engine, gRPC (`google.golang.org/grpc`), Protocol Buffers (`google.golang.org/protobuf`), testify (`github.com/stretchr/testify`). Internal: `internal/config` (translation configuration), `internal/ingest` (OTLP handlers and translator), `internal/query` (Prometheus query API), `internal/store` (metric storage backends). Build constraint: `//go:build integration` tag keeps integration tests separate from unit tests.
sections.files:
| File | Purpose |
|------|----------|
| `integration_test.go` | Single file containing all integration tests with `//go:build integration` tag. Contains: `testServer` struct and helpers, 12 test functions covering ingestion protocols/metric types/storage backends/concurrency/persistence/error cases, and utility functions for query execution and response parsing. Tests are parallel-safe with ephemeral port allocation.
